13	// LivenessReporter observes worker liveness ticks. WithHeartbeat invokes
14	// OnTick after each TouchClaimHeartbeat so the caller can mirror the same
15	// signal to a worker-status sidecar and to the optional server probe
16	// without rewriting the bead tracker.
17	type LivenessReporter interface {
18		OnTick(now time.Time)
19	}
20	
21	// SidecarLivenessReporter is the production LivenessReporter. Each tick it
22	// (a) writes a status.json sidecar under .ddx/workers/<worker-id>/ via the
23	// workerstatus package, and (b) emits a worker.heartbeat envelope to sink
24	// (typically the loop EventSink, which is teed to the workerprobe). The
25	// sidecar write is best-effort and never fails the loop; the sink emit is
26	// best-effort and never blocks the heartbeat goroutine.
27	type SidecarLivenessReporter struct {
28		mu          sync.Mutex
29		projectRoot string
30		rec         workerstatus.LivenessRecord
31		sink        io.Writer
32		sessionID   string
33	}
34	
35	// NewSidecarLivenessReporter constructs a SidecarLivenessReporter for the
36	// given worker. projectRoot must be the project's git root (where .ddx/
37	// lives). sink may be nil for callers that don't want event mirroring.
38	func NewSidecarLivenessReporter(projectRoot, workerID, sessionID string, sink io.Writer) *SidecarLivenessReporter {
39		return &SidecarLivenessReporter{
40			projectRoot: projectRoot,
41			sink:        sink,
42			sessionID:   sessionID,
43			rec: workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
44				WorkerID:    workerID,
45				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
46				PID:         os.Getpid(),
47				StartedAt:   time.Now().UTC(),
48			},
49		}
50	}
51	
52	// SetAttempt records the in-flight attempt identity. Called once per claim
53	// before WithHeartbeat starts so the first tick records the right bead.
54	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) SetAttempt(beadID, attemptID, phase, route, harness, model, profile string, childPID int) {
55		if r == nil {
56			return
57		}
58		r.mu.Lock()
59		r.rec.CurrentBead = beadID
60		r.rec.AttemptID = attemptID
61		r.rec.Phase = phase
62		r.rec.Route = route
63		r.rec.Harness = harness
64		r.rec.Model = model
65		r.rec.Profile = profile
66		r.rec.ChildPID = childPID
67		r.mu.Unlock()
68	}
69	
70	// ClearAttempt drops the in-flight attempt identity once the attempt
71	// terminates. The worker_id and project_root remain so an idle drain loop
72	// still presents as the same worker between attempts.
73	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) ClearAttempt() {
74		if r == nil {
75			return
76		}
77		r.mu.Lock()
78		r.rec.CurrentBead = ""
79		r.rec.AttemptID = ""
80		r.rec.Phase = ""
81		r.rec.ChildPID = 0
82		r.mu.Unlock()
83	}
84	
85	// OnTick updates the sidecar with the new last_activity_at and mirrors a
86	// worker.heartbeat envelope to the configured sink (best-effort).
87	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) OnTick(now time.Time) {
88		if r == nil {
89			return
90		}
91		r.mu.Lock()
92		r.rec.LastActivityAt = now.UTC()
93		snapshot := r.rec
94		r.mu.Unlock()
95		_ = workerstatus.WriteLiveness(r.projectRoot, snapshot.WorkerID, snapshot)
96		r.emit(snapshot, now)
97	}
98	
99	// Snapshot returns a copy of the current sidecar record.
100	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) Snapshot() workerstatus.LivenessRecord {
101		r.mu.Lock()
102		defer r.mu.Unlock()
103		return r.rec
104	}
105	
106	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) emit(rec workerstatus.LivenessRecord, now time.Time) {
107		if r.sink == nil {
108			return
109		}
110		entry := map[string]any{
111			"session_id": r.sessionID,
112			"type":       "worker.heartbeat",
113			"ts":         now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
114			"data": map[string]any{
115				"worker_id":        rec.WorkerID,
116				"bead_id":          rec.CurrentBead,
117				"attempt_id":       rec.AttemptID,
118				"phase":            rec.Phase,
119				"route":            rec.Route,
120				"harness":          rec.Harness,
121				"model":            rec.Model,
122				"profile":          rec.Profile,
123				"last_activity_at": rec.LastActivityAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
124			},
125		}
126		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
127		if err != nil {
128			return
129		}
130		line = append(line, '\n')
131		_, _ = r.sink.Write(line)
132	}

19		ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
20		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
21		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
22		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
23		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted

26	// RunArgs holds options for a single agent invocation.
27	type RunArgs struct {
28		// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
29		// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
30		// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
31		// Nil defaults to context.Background().
32		Context       context.Context
33		Harness       string
34		Prompt        string // prompt text (or path to file)
35		PromptFile    string // explicit file path
36		PromptSource  string
37		Correlation   map[string]string
38		Model         string
39		Provider      string // explicit provider name (e.g. "vidar", "openrouter"); bypasses default provider selection
40		Effort        string
41		Timeout       time.Duration // idle (inactivity) timeout; nonzero overrides Config.TimeoutMS
42		WallClock     time.Duration // absolute wall-clock cap; nonzero overrides Config.WallClockMS
43		WorkDir       string
44		Permissions   string // permission level override: safe, supervised, unrestricted
45		SessionLogDir string // per-run override for session log dir; used by execute-bead to redirect embedded-agent runtime state out of the worktree root
46		Role          string
47		CorrelationID string
48		Env           map[string]string

51	// AgentRunRuntime is the SD-024 successor to RunArgs for the agent
52	// run path. Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, Effort,
53	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
54	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
55	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
56	// runtime intent remain.
57	//
58	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
59	type AgentRunRuntime struct {
60		Prompt                string
61		PromptFile            string
62		PromptSource          string
63		Output                io.Writer // optional live service-event progress sink
64		WorkLogPhase          string    // optional phase label for live service-event progress
65		Correlation           map[string]string
66		Record                bool
67		Replay                bool
68		WorkDir               string
69		EstimatedPromptTokens int
70		RequiresTools         bool
71		SessionLogDirOverride string
72		// HarnessOverride, ProviderOverride, ModelOverride, ProfileOverride, and
73		// PermissionsOverride let a caller override one durable knob from rcfg for
74		// this single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
75		// SD-024 step B22d-d: execute-bead sets permissions=unrestricted; hidden
76		// lifecycle calls use profile-only routing hints sourced from fizeau.
77		HarnessOverride     string
78		ProviderOverride    string
79		ModelOverride       string
80		ProfileOverride     string
81		PermissionsOverride string
82		// ClearRoutingPins drops rcfg Harness/Provider/Model passthrough for
83		// hidden DDx lifecycle calls. Explicit runtime overrides still win.
84		ClearRoutingPins bool
85		Role             string
86		CorrelationID    string
87		Env              map[string]string
88		// MinPowerOverride, when > 0, overrides rcfg.MinPower() for this single
89		// invocation. Used by the post-merge reviewer to pin MinPower above the
90		// implementer's actual selected power, biasing routing toward a stronger
91		// reviewer model (R4 pairing: reviewer >= impl + 1 power).
92		MinPowerOverride int
93		// ClearMinPower drops rcfg.MinPower() for this single invocation. Auxiliary
94		// lifecycle classifier calls should not inherit implementation power bounds;
95		// Fizeau owns routing for those hidden dispatches.
96		ClearMinPower bool
97		// ClearMaxPower drops rcfg.MaxPower() for this single invocation. This is
98		// for auxiliary classifier/reviewer-style calls that deliberately want the
99		// strongest viable route instead of the worker attempt's cost cap.
100		ClearMaxPower bool
101		// ClearProfile drops rcfg.Profile() for this single invocation. Auxiliary
102		// calls select their own fizeau profile hints instead of inheriting the
103		// worker attempt's profile.
104		ClearProfile bool
105	}

29	// logic: a non-empty route field resets the phase-empty timer, and the
30	// watchdog only fires once the phase-empty signature has persisted past the
31	// applicable budget.
32	func TestProgressWatchdogObserveResetsOnProgress(t *testing.T) {
33		wd := &progressWatchdog{budgets: PhaseBudgets{Resolving: time.Minute, Running: 10 * time.Minute}}
34		base := time.Date(2026, 5, 26, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)

41		// A heartbeat that carries harness+model is forward progress: reset.
42		fired, _ = wd.observe(base.Add(20*time.Second), "running", "claude", "opus", "balanced")
43		require.False(t, fired)
44	
45		// Back to phase-empty: the timer restarts from this observation, so even
46		// well past the original start it has not yet crossed the budget.
47		fired, _ = wd.observe(base.Add(30*time.Second), "running", "", "", "")
48		require.False(t, fired)

51		fired, _ = wd.observe(base.Add(30*time.Second+11*time.Minute), "running", "", "", "")

63		base := time.Date(2026, 5, 26, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
64		var clockNanos atomic.Int64
65		clockNanos.Store(base.UnixNano())
66		nowFn := func() time.Time { return time.Unix(0, clockNanos.Load()).UTC() }

76				Now:     nowFn,
